theme: Place allocations that can never be moved
title: Cold Vault: Place What Can Never Move
strategies: fresh-slab / bump allocator: give every block a private, ever-growing byte range and never reuse a freed one (reproduces the evaluator's weak fresh-slab baseline)
arrival-order first-fit: process blocks in birth order and drop each at the lowest address free of every currently-alive block, reusing holes but blind to future death times

lifetime-keyed placement: process blocks in an order keyed on death time (segregating the longest-lived 'spine' low, packing each equal-death cohort together) so cohorts vacate one contiguous hole per stage instead of many stranded fragments
multi-order search: try several lifetime-based processing orders (death-desc, longevity-desc, explicit spine/cohort split, death-asc, arrival) and keep whichever yields the lowest high-water mark
